edible_parts: Flowers, young flower stalk, and fruit (cooked); fiber/soap.
other_uses: Tall trunk-forming desert yucca with fine grassy leaves and towering cream flower spikes.
prep: Cook the flowers and young emerging flower stalk (asparagus-like) and the green fruit; the leaves make cordage/baskets and the roots ('amole') a soap. A signature Chihuahuan Desert plant.
risks: Leaf tips sharp; roots soapy (not eaten).
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
